description: Internal sales performance dashboard — call volume, show rates, qualification, lead source attribution, compliance tracking

project_overview.what: The QS Revenue Dashboard pulls sales data from GoHighLevel (CRM) and turns it into a real-time performance dashboard. It tracks call volume, show rates, close rates, lead source attribution, rep-level performance, and compliance for Quantum Scaling's sales team.
project_overview.why: Lloyd needs to see exactly how each sales rep is performing, which lead sources are producing revenue, and where the pipeline is leaking — without manually pulling GHL reports. This is the operational nervous system for the sales org.
project_overview.end_state: A comprehensive revenue intelligence platform with real-time KPIs, financial integration (Xero for actual revenue vs pipeline), automated anomaly detection, and API endpoints that feed the OpenClaw agent layer for cross-app synthesis.
project_overview.current_summary: Dashboard is fully live and in daily use. Shows key sales metrics at a glance — call volume, show rates, close rates, and lead source performance. Each rep's numbers are clickable to see the specific deals behind them. Compliance tracking flags reps who aren't logging outcomes or notes. Sales data syncs automatically from GoHighLevel every 15 minutes, and the backend is ready for other tools to pull data from it.

resume_here: Dashboard is live and usable. Phase 1 feature work is largely complete. Next priority is Phase 2 (Xero/WAP financial integration) or additional Phase 1 polish items (WoW deltas on 7-day table, export features). Railway deploy workflow: push to `railway` remote via `git push railway $(git subtree split --prefix=qs-dashboard HEAD):main --force` — do NOT rely on `origin` push for qs-dashboard deploys.

current_phase.name: Phase 1 - Live & Iterating
current_phase.goal: Full internal dashboard for Lloyd to monitor sales team performance: call volume per rep, show rates, qualification breakdowns, lead source attribution, compliance tracking
current_focus: Phase 1 feature complete — dashboard live and in daily use
Minor UX additions as needed (week navigation on 7-day table just shipped)
immediate_next_steps: Confirm week navigation working correctly after latest deploy
Remove diagnostic v3 marker from dashboard header (cosmetic cleanup)
Evaluate readiness for Phase 2 (Xero financial integration)

known_issues.title: Railway deploy requires manual subtree push — origin push does NOT redeploy qs-dashboard
known_issues.resolution: qs-dashboard service watches `qs-revenue-dashboard` repo (separate from monorepo). Deploy cmd: git push railway $(git subtree split --prefix=qs-dashboard HEAD):main --force

known_issues.title: Diagnostic v3 marker visible in dashboard header
known_issues.resolution: Remove `v3` from header in dashboard.html and redeploy

next_phase.name: Phase 2 - Financial Integration
next_phase.goal: Connect Xero and WAP for payment processing and cash collection visibility alongside sales performance
key_features: Xero API integration for revenue/payment data
WAP integration for cash collection tracking
Combined view: sales pipeline → payment → cash collected
next_phase.prerequisites: Phase 1 confirmed stable in daily use. KPI definitions validated by Lloyd against real team data.

future_phases.name: Phase 3 - Advanced Analytics
future_phases.goal: Deeper metric slicing, predictive insights, stronger management views, trend analysis
future_phases.prerequisites: Enough historical data for trends to be meaningful (4-6 weeks of synced data minimum).

recent_milestones.description: Weekly activity view now lets you browse previous weeks — see day-by-day bookings, shows, and qualifications for any week
Fixed production deployment process so dashboard updates go live reliably
"Not Logged" column is now clickable — shows exactly which deals each rep hasn't updated with an outcome
Added a day-by-day activity breakdown showing bookings, shows, and qualifications for the current week
Rep performance numbers are now clickable — see the specific deals behind each rep's bookings, shows, and closes, with direct links to GoHighLevel
Compliance tracking is live — see which reps aren't logging outcomes, which ones are writing low-quality notes, and disqualification rates per rep
Dashboard backend is now ready for other tools to pull sales data and insights from it via API
Dashboard launched — key metrics, show rate trends, sales funnel visualization, and per-rep performance table all visible at a glance
First complete data sync from GoHighLevel — pulled in 3,243 deals with zero errors
Backend went live — sales data now syncs automatically from GoHighLevel every 15 minutes

decision_log.decision: qs-dashboard lives in monorepo (compete-iq) locally but deploys from standalone qs-revenue-dashboard GitHub repo
decision_log.reasoning: Railway service was originally created from the standalone repo. Easiest to maintain this — use git subtree push to keep qs-revenue-dashboard in sync with monorepo changes.

decision_log.decision: Compliance sync runs every 15 minutes via APScheduler
decision_log.reasoning: Lloyd wanted near-real-time visibility into outcome-not-logged violations so reps can be corrected same-day. 15 min cadence is fast enough without hammering GHL API.

decision_log.decision: Split compliance into outcome_unfilled (no show/no-show logged) vs note compliance (note missing or low word count)
decision_log.reasoning: These are different failure modes requiring different coaching responses. Outcome unfilled = rep forgot to log. Note noncompliant = rep logged but didn't document.
GHL assigns opportunity owner to the rep who created/owns the deal. This is the most reliable attribution signal.

decision_log.decision: Show/no-show detection via union of appointment status + pipeline stage signals
decision_log.reasoning: No single GHL field reliably indicates show/no-show. Union of signals catches more cases than either alone.
Avoids N+1 fetch to get appointment dates. Custom fields on Opportunity allow single-query access.

decision_log.decision: Bearer token auth on all API endpoints
decision_log.reasoning: Simple, sufficient for internal tool. Upgrade to JWT/API key scoping when cross-app orchestration needs differentiated access.
